patches.find: (Mount Meru is north of a spherical earth in Hindu cosmology; Yggdrasil connects nine realms with no single spatial axis; Mount Olympus is at 40°N in Greek geography; Mount Qaf encircles the boundary with no interior center)
patches.replace: (Mount Meru is at the center of a flat disc in Puranic Hindu cosmology, or at the geographic north pole of a spherical earth in the Aryabhatan astronomical tradition; Yggdrasil is a vertical world-tree connecting nine realms across three tiers with no specified earth-geometry beneath it; Mount Qaf encircles the world's boundary with no interior center; the Mesopotamian Ḫursag / E-kur stands at the cosmic center of a flat-earth model)
patches.rationale: ISS-1817/1818/1819 combined: drop Olympus (not a cosmologically-positioned axis-mundi, just where gods live — category error); fix Meru (Puranic dominant tradition is flat-disc center, not Aryabhatan north-pole); fix Yggdrasil (IS a single vertical axis — world-tree; restate as 'no specified earth-geometry beneath it'); add Mesopotamian Hursag/E-kur as a stricter cosmological axis-mundi replacement for Olympus.

patches.find: all texts predate the model by centuries to millennia
patches.replace: all texts predate the model by two to three millennia
patches.rationale: ISS-1820: harmonize detail_verdict_text with detail_evidence and tldr_verdict, which both say 'two to three millennia'. The lower bound 'centuries' is wrong — even 1 Enoch (~100 BCE) is ~21 centuries old.

patches.find: the resulting fraction would be approximately the same as the current 94.5%, because the carve-out removes claims from both numerator and denominator simultaneously
patches.replace: the resulting fraction (66/70 = 94.3% if WIN-031, 032, and 034 are all carved out) would be approximately the same as the current 94.5%, because the carve-out removes claims from both numerator and denominator simultaneously
patches.rationale: ISS-1821: name the specific corrected fraction so a defender cannot score a free rhetorical hit by asking 'well what is it exactly?'

patches.find: monitor.py — 39 domain dictionaries at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 currently active against status_history.json
patches.replace: monitor.py, 28 active domains against status_history.json; 39 defined at commit 8e4bf9b
patches.rationale: ISS-1377: clearer ordering — lead with active count (28), note defined count (39) second. Removes em-dash inside parens (ISS-1378 style concern also addressed by this rephrasing).
